questions.prompt: Under ORS 701.026, when may a contractor hire another contractor as a subcontractor?
choices: Only when the hired party is properly licensed/endorsed or exempt under ORS 701.010
Any time there is a written subcontract
Only if the project is residential
Only if the subcontractor has workers' compensation coverage
questions.explanation: ORS 701.026 focuses on licensing compliance: the hired contractor must be licensed and properly endorsed for the work unless an ORS 701.010 exemption applies.

questions.prompt: Which statute is the best starting point to verify whether an exemption from CCB licensure might apply?
questions.explanation: ORS 701.010 contains the exemption framework. ORS 701.021 and ORS 701.026 establish core licensing duties, while ORS 670.600 addresses independent-contractor criteria.

questions.prompt: The PSI Oregon CCB bulletin indicates that 'Working with Subcontractors' accounts for how many scored items?
questions.explanation: The reviewed PSI Candidate Information Bulletin allocates 2 scored items to Working with Subcontractors out of 80 scored items total.
